topStories.title: India's private banks draw thinner margins on tepid credit growth
topStories.summary: Top private sector banks reported weaker net interest margins (NIMs) in the June quarter as sluggish retail loan demand pushed them towards lower-yielding corporate loans. HDFC Bank's NIM fell to 3.26%, Kotak Mahindra Bank's dropped to a 19-quarter low of 4.53%, while Axis Bank's stood at 3.46%.

topStories.title: Panchayat, local body elections in Rajasthan to be completed by November 15
topStories.summary: The Bharatiya Janata Party government submitted the schedule for panchayat and local body elections in the Rajasthan High Court during the hearing on a batch of public interest petitions

topStories.title: Six killed in floods, landslips in Northeast
topStories.summary: Railway authorities cancelled, diverted, or short-terminated several passenger train services in Assam. At least 25 people have been killed across four States since the last week of June

topStories.summary: UK's new PM Andy Burnham named ex-health minister Wes Streeting as his defence secretary late Monday.

topStories.summary: Among his many achievements, Sobers's 365 not out against Pakistan in 1958 stood as the highest individual innings in Test cricket for 36 years

topStories.summary: His death was announced on July 20 by his former club Newcastle, where he had been both a player and coach. Keegan revealed last month that he had stage four cancer

topStories.title: US stocks today: S&P 500 edges down with Iran and earnings season in focus
topStories.summary: U.S. stocks closed slightly lower as investors balanced Middle East tensions with optimism around upcoming tech earnings. Markets remained cautious amid US-Iran developments and oil supply concerns, while attention shifts to results from major firms like Alphabet and Intel, expected to shape sentiment on corporate health and future growth outlook.

india.title: Meet Sarah Kendzior: The bestselling author who traded her writing desk for a $17-an-hour garden centre job and found unexpected joy
Mediators propose 10-day truce to revive interim deal: Iran official
india.summary: Iran received a 10-day ceasefire proposal from mediators to end the ongoing conflict. US strikes on Iranian cities continued for a ninth consecutive night. Iran's Revolutionary Guards attacked US military assets across the region. Two oil tankers exploded after attempting to transit the Strait of Hormuz. A vessel was struck by an unknown projectile off Oman's coast.

india.title: Have demanded extradition of Hasina aide from India: Bangladesh minister
india.summary: Bangladesh has requested India to extradite former minister Asaduzzaman Khan Kamal. He fled the country amid the "2024 uprising" against the government. Kamal was sentenced to death for crimes against humanity by a tribunal. Dhaka also expects India to respond to extradition requests for other fugitives. The government stated that Hasina would face legal consequences if she surrenders.

india.title: Quote of the Day by Seneca: 'We suffer more often in imagination than in reality'
india.summary: Seneca, the ancient Stoic philosopher, observed that our imagination often causes us more suffering than actual events. His timeless insight resonates with issues of anxiety faced today. Contemporary research shows our brain struggles to differentiate between real threats and imagined ones. Cognitive Behavioral Therapy leverages this understanding to help individuals challenge distorted thoughts, enabling them to tackle fears more effectively and manage anxiety around potential issues.

india.title: Looksmaxxing creator Hullo reportedly returns to TikTok after 15 CSAM charges
india.summary: Mason Hull, known online as Hullo, has reportedly resumed posting on TikTok. This comeback follows his recent charges for child sexual abuse material possession. Many social media users are discussing his return and platform policies. His legal case remains ongoing, with no verdict reached yet. The creator's online presence continues to spark debate among the public.

india.title: Quote of the day by Epicurus: 'Death does not concern us, because as long as we exist...'
india.summary: Epicurus, the ancient Greek philosopher, offered a compelling perspective on the fear of death, asserting that it should not concern us since our existence ceases when death arrives. This notion, termed the symmetry argument, challenges the fears associated with dying. Residing in a community known as The Garden, Epicurus sought to promote tranquility through his teachings, which continue to resonate today amidst contemporary worries about life and health.

india.title: Quote of the day by Usha Vance: 'There's nothing more exciting than getting to see the world through the eye of kids as they're growing up' and the joys of motherhood
Scientists tracked endangered Australian cockatoos for three breeding seasons and found...
india.summary: The peril faced by Carnaby's black cockatoos is escalating, as pesticide contamination in their food reaches alarming rates. Testing of seed samples revealed that toxic chemicals present are hundreds of times higher than what is legally permissible. This exposure might shed light on the troubling paralysis syndrome afflicting these endangered birds. Furthermore, researchers identified neurotoxic substances banned in various regions of Europe.

tn.summary: Since June 1, the start of the current water year, four reservoirs of the Cauvery basin have received around 30 tmc ft, whereas Tamil Nadu has not even received four tmc ft, says an expert in Chennai

tn.title: Tamil Nadu Law Minister R. Nirmalkumar justifies the arrest of DMK Vilathikulam MLA G.V. Markandayan
tn.summary: He says the MLA spoke in a threatening manner. If an MLA goes to the extent of issuing a death threat to the Chief Minister, it is definitely a law and order issue. If someone speaks in this manner, legal action is bound to be taken.

tn.title: Bhaktavatsalam’s take on the destruction of documents relating to anti-Hindi agitation in Tamil Nadu
tn.summary: He said he was advised that papers on politicians against whom detention orders had been served need not be kept so that they would not be available to the very people who were likely to take charge of the government. When Ministers came to know about officers who had made reports about them, they would get prejudiced, he further said

tn.title: Meenambal Sivaraj: a trailblazer for Dalit rights and women empowerment
tn.summary: Meenambal Sivaraj was born on December 12, 1904, in Rangoon, in a family that placed great importance on education and service. One of her most significant contributions was her work with All India Scheduled Castes Federation founded by Ambedkar

tn.title: The 1995 caste reprisals that scarred southern T.N. also touched Communist leader Nallakannu’s life
tn.summary: On December 3, 1995, Nallakannu’s 84-year-old father-in-law, A.K. Annasamy, was murdered by a gang while he was asleep at his house in Marudanvalvu village, then part of Chidambaranar district (now Thoothukudi district)

tn.title: Dolphin City: Chennai’s dolphinarium that shaped national debate on marine mammal captivity over 25 years ago
tn.summary: Hosting a dolphinarium in Chennai turned into controversy as all the three dolphins died, setting off one of the country’s earliest debates on the ethics of marine mammal captivity

trichy.summary: The bund of the canal, which is maintained by the Water Resources Department, is ideal to form the road and the government could even acquire private landholdings for the purpose, say activists

trichy.title: The Kumbakonam connection of MGR, M.K. Nambyar and M.S. Swaminathan
trichy.summary: The families of the iconic film star and former Tamil Nadu Chief Minister, the distinguished constitutional lawyer, and the celebrated agricultural scientist hailed from Kerala. But they spent a formative part of their lives in Kumbakonam, known for its rich cultural heritage, before rising to national prominence in politics, cinema, law, and science

trichy.title: An idol found near Gangaikonda Cholapuram confirms the ‘Thiruvarasu’ of Nathamunigal
trichy.summary: Near the Gangaikonda Cholapuram-Kumbakonam Road is a place called Sorgapallam or Sembodai where an idol of Srinivasa Perumal as once found half-buried under a tree. In the year 2000, another idol was found buried in a field. It was that of the first of the Sri Vaishnava Acharyas, who was said to have been at the place in his final hours

trichy.title: Thanjavur Big Temple inscriptions bring alive the fierce fight between Shivaji and Afzal Khan
trichy.summary: The inscriptions, written in Marathi on the south-western walls near the Vinayagar Temple, also describe the history of the Maratha kings and praise Danish missionary Rev. Friedrich Christian Schwartz. They also highlight how Serfoji sent his army and offered financial assistance to the British during the war against the Marudu brothers

trichy.title: J.C. Daniel, a Tamil dentist and martial artist, who became the father of Malayalam cinema
trichy.summary: Driven by his passion for cinema, Daniel sold his share of family property in Kanniyakumari district and borrowed money from friends and relatives to produce the first Malayalam silent film Vigathakumaran (The Lost Child). The film told the story of a child kidnapped by a plantation worker from Sri Lanka and later reunited with his family

trichy.title: Collection of names of animals as portrayed in Tamil classics
trichy.summary: The ancient Tamils employed a remarkable richness of vocabulary to refer to animals. H. Chithiraputhiran, a former professor at Tamil University, Thanjavur, has compiled a specialised dictionary of animals in classical literature to recover these words

trichy.title: Goddess Saraswati without veena: Sculptures of Pallava, Chola eras reveal an earlier iconography
trichy.summary: The veena, today regarded as inseparable from Saraswati’s identity, appears to have emerged as her dominant symbol only in the later period.

business.title: India's private banks draw thinner margins on tepid credit growth
business.summary: Top private sector banks reported weaker net interest margins (NIMs) in the June quarter as sluggish retail loan demand pushed them towards lower-yielding corporate loans. HDFC Bank's NIM fell to 3.26%, Kotak Mahindra Bank's dropped to a 19-quarter low of 4.53%, while Axis Bank's stood at 3.46%.

business.title: US stocks today: S&P 500 edges down with Iran and earnings season in focus
business.summary: U.S. stocks closed slightly lower as investors balanced Middle East tensions with optimism around upcoming tech earnings. Markets remained cautious amid US-Iran developments and oil supply concerns, while attention shifts to results from major firms like Alphabet and Intel, expected to shape sentiment on corporate health and future growth outlook.

business.title: Paytm Q1 Results: Profit soars 79% YoY to Rs 220 crore; board decides not to go with bonus proposal
business.summary: Paytm's net profit surged seventy-nine percent year-on-year to two hundred twenty crore rupees. The company's board decided against a bonus issue at this time. They will continue focusing on compounding growth and profitability for shareholders. This decision aims to enhance long-term shareholder value creation. The company emphasized its commitment to sustained financial performance.

This significant move involves its majority-owned Indian bottling partner, Hindustan Coca-Cola Holdings.  The beverage giant is preparing for this listing and exploring a partial stake sale.  This strategy aligns with other global firms tapping into India's growing equity markets.  The IPO aims to monetize investments, attracting valuations richer than domestic markets.
